response.justification: Candidate demonstrates strong technical leadership, large-scale infrastructure experience, and proven mentoring ability through staff-level roles at Google and Meta. However, they critically fall short of the minimum 8-year C++ requirement (has approximately 1-2 years total), which is a stated must-have. While their systems expertise and recent senior positions suggest capability to grow into the role, the C++ gap and lack of specialized cloud compute platform/DevOps infrastructure focus present significant hurdles. Worth reconsidering only if C++ is not a hard requirement or if the company can invest in training.
strengths: Extensive large-scale distributed systems experience (Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second, Meta infrastructure platforms)
Demonstrated technical leadership through staff roles at Google and Meta plus community mentoring via workshops and teaching
Strong cloud architecture and infrastructure background (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker, production cloud deployment)
concerns: Severely inadequate C++ experience (~1-2 years vs 8-year requirement); C++ only appears in 2014-2015 at National Instruments and early freelance work
No specialized focus on cloud compute platforms or DevOps infrastructure tooling—experience is broad full-stack rather than deep infrastructure
Educational background is Photography/Video/Image Processing (2006-2009) plus relatively recent CS degree (2009-2012); no Master's or PhD in engineering/CS
